title: WebAssembly.validate probes run twice on first getCapabilities() call due to computeCapabilities calling probes then detectTier
description: computeCapabilities() calls _probeSimd() at line 222, _probeRelaxedSimd() at line 224, and then calls detectTier() at line 256. Because _cachedTier is not yet set when computeCapabilities runs, detectTier() re-runs all the same WebAssembly.validate probes from scratch (lines 95-107 of detectTier). On the relaxed-simd-mt path this means _probeSimd() and _probeRelaxedSimd() are each called twice per first getCapabilities() invocation. WebAssembly.validate is synchronous and fast, but these are unnecessary duplicate calls. Fix: call detectTier() first in computeCapabilities and derive wasmSimd/wasmRelaxedSimd from the already-cached tier result, or cache individual probe results at module level.

title: Individual probe results (_probeSimd, _probeRelaxedSimd, etc.) are not cached at module level
description: _probeSimd(), _probeRelaxedSimd(), _probeWasmThreads(), and _probeWasmExceptions() each call WebAssembly.validate on every invocation with no result memoization. The only memoization is at the detectTier() and getCapabilities() level. If _resetCache() is called (e.g. in tests), or if detectTier() and computeCapabilities() are called in an order that causes double probing (see performance-1), the underlying validate calls repeat unnecessarily. Caching the boolean result of each probe at module level (similar to how _cachedTier is stored) would eliminate all duplicate validate calls and make _resetCache behavior explicit.
